SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT AND COMPLT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF DEATON, LEONORE SERVED AUG-31-2007, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON CORPORATION AS TO DEFENDANT 16TH & DELORES ASSOCIATES L.P.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT 16TH & DELORES ASSOCIATES L.P.'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - THE MOTION IS DENIED. CCP SECTION 425.16 MAY BE INVOKED AS IT RELATES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND POSSIBLY THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE OF THE RELIANCE ON THE NOTICE OF TERMINATION. HOWEVER, THE THRUST OF THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT IS ON PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO QUIET ENJOYMENT AND HABITABLE PREMISES. TO THE EXTENT CCP SECTION 425.16 IS APPLICABLE, PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING BY POINTING THE EXISTENCE OF FACTS IN SUPPORT OF HER CLAIM BY DECLARATION AND BY REFERENCE TO DOCUMENTS WHICH CERTAINLY WILL BE ADMISSIBLE AT THE TIME OF TRIAL. TO THE EXTENT EVIDENCE HAS BEEN PRESENTED IN OPPOSITION THAT MAY NOT CONFORM TO ALL OF THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE EVIDENCE CODE, IT IS OBVIOUS THAT PLAINTIFF CAN OVERCOME THE OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS D, E, F, I, J, K. THE DECLARATIONS, EXHIBITS A, B AND C ARE ADMISSIBLE. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF LEONORE DEATON'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PER CCP 664.6 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE OF A SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT THAT MAY BE ENFORCED UNDER CCP 664.6. NO EVIDENCE OF A WRITTEN AGREEMENT SIGNED BY THE PARTIES OR AGREED TO BY THE PARTIES ON THE RECORD BEFORE THE COURT HAS BEEN SHOWN. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Per Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Sep-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF LEONORE DEATON'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PER CCP 664.6 is denied. Plaintiff has failed to present evidence of a settlement agreement that may be enforced under CCP 664.6. No evidence of a written agreement signed by the parties or agreed to by the parties on the record before the Court has been shown. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Deft'S Special Motion To Strike Complaint;Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Nov-01-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT 16TH & DELORES ASSOCIATES L.P.'S Special Motion To Strike Complaint.
APPEARANCES: Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq. 533-0735 of Law Offices of Mary Catherine Wiederhold & Eric J. Farber, Esq. of Farber & Co. Attorneys LLP 434-5320 x105 for pltf & opposing party; Ray Z. Bacerdo, Esq. of Drath Clifford Murphy & Hagen LLP 510/287-4000 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE MOTION IS DENIED. CCP SECTION 425.16 MAY BE INVOKED AS IT RELATES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND POSSIBLY THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE OF THE RELIANCE ON THE NOTICE OF TERMINATION. HOWEVER, THE THRUST OF THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT IS ON PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO QUIET ENJOYMENT AND HABITABLE PREMISES. TO THE EXTENT CCP SECTION 425.16 IS APPLICABLE, PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING BY POINTING THE EXISTENCE OF FACTS IN SUPPORT OF HER CLAIM BY DECLARATION AND BY REFERENCE TO DOCUMENTS WHICH CERTAINLY WILL BE ADMISSIBLE AT THE TIME OF TRIAL. TO THE EXTENT EVIDENCE HAS BEEN PRESENTED IN OPPOSITION THAT MAY NOT CONFORM TO ALL OF THE REQUIREMENTS OF THE EVIDENCE CODE, IT IS OBVIOUS THAT PLAINTIFF CAN OVERCOME THE OBJECTIONS TO EXHIBITS D, E, F, I, J, K. THE DECLARATIONS, EXHIBITS A, B AND C ARE ADMISSIBLE. (302/PJM/SN-X) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
